spec_match.rationale: The page now logs `beforeinput.dataTransfer` for contrast and reads `input.dataTransfer` to verify the custom `application/x-attribution` MIME on the committed drop event.

interactive.rationale: Draggable research-chip palette with a real contenteditable drop target; custom application/x-attribution MIME survives the dataTransfer round-trip; log shows the inferred behaviour.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept and feature blurbs now frame beforeinput as the contrast path and `input.dataTransfer` as the v143 capability.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles null input-side dataTransfer and missing custom MIME, and converts the inserted text with Range APIs. It still does not cover drops from outside the page.

design_system.rationale: Variables only; no raw hex in script.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'drop from outside the page' route
openQuestions.detail: Dragging files or text from another window/app is the most common drop in editors. A drop zone that accepts an OS-level drag (text URL list, file with text content) would prove the dataTransfer plumbing works for cross-origin sources, not just internal chip-to-editor drags.

openQuestions.title: Escape the cite URL before injection
openQuestions.detail: `escapedCite` only replaces &<>. Cite URLs can contain `"` which would break the rendered HTML if it ever ended up inside an attribute (today it doesn't — it's only in text — but the escape table should still match the standard escapeHTML defined in CLAUDE.md rule #4, which also escapes `"` and `'`).

summary: The chip-to-editor drag now demonstrates the v143 path: beforeinput logs the incoming payload, input.dataTransfer verifies the custom MIME, and the inserted text is replaced with a cited block quote using Range APIs. External OS/app drops remain a useful expansion.
